question: Identify a specific California county based on a set of health and housing statistics. The county had an Area Median Income of at least $142,800 in 2022 and $147,900 in 2023. It also had a women & infants & children average cost per family of $570 or more in 2018 and $700 or more in 2020, a 2015 perioperative hemorrhage/hematoma observed rate of 19 or higher, and a geographical shape area of at least 3 billion square meters. What county matches all these criteria? Only report the county name. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/income-limits-by-county/files/2022-income-limits.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
df["AMI"] = pd.to_numeric(df["AMI"], errors="coerce")
filtered = df[df["AMI"] >= 142800].copy().sort_values("AMI", ascending=False)
answer = {str(row["County"]).strip(): int(row["AMI"]) for _, row in filtered.iterrows()}
1.subquestion: Which counties have AMI >= 142800 in the 2022 income limits data? (Filter: AMI >= 142800; list County and AMI.)

3.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/california-women-infants-and-children-program-redemption-by-county-967e9/files/2010-2018-part-a-wic-redemptions-by-vendor-county-with-family-counts.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
node_1_answer = {'Santa Clara': 168500, 'Marin': 166000, 'San Francisco': 166000, 'San Mateo': 166000, 'Alameda': 142800, 'Contra Costa': 142800}
node_2_answer = {'Santa Clara': 181300, 'Marin': 175000, 'San Francisco': 175000, 'San Mateo': 175000, 'Alameda': 147900, 'Contra Costa': 147900}

county_set = set(str(k).strip() for k in dict(node_1_answer).keys()) | set(str(k).strip() for k in dict(node_2_answer).keys())
df["Obligation Year and Month"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Obligation Year and Month"], errors="coerce")
filtered = df[df["Obligation Year and Month"].eq(2018)].copy()
filtered["Vendor County"] = filtered["Vendor County"].astype(str).str.strip()
filtered = filtered[filtered["Vendor County"].isin(county_set)]
filtered["Average Cost per Family"] = pd.to_numeric(
    filtered["Average Cost per Family"].astype(str).str.replace(r"[^0-9.\-]", "", regex=True),
    errors="coerce",
)
answer = sorted(filtered[filtered["Average Cost per Family"] >= 570]["Vendor County"].dropna().astype(str).unique().tolist())
3.subquestion: Among the counties from hop 1, which have Average Cost per Family >= $570 in the 2018 WIC redemptions data? (Filter: Obligation Year and Month == 2018; Vendor County in <node_1 answer>, <node_2 answer>; parse Average Cost per Family; keep >= 570.)

5.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/hospitalization-counts-and-rates-of-selected-adverse-hospital-events-by-california-county-67a4a/files/ca-oshpd-adveventhospitalizationspsi-county2005-2015q3.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
node_3_answer = ['Alameda', 'Contra Costa', 'San Francisco', 'Santa Clara']
node_4_answer = ['Alameda', 'San Francisco', 'San Mateo', 'Santa Clara']


county_set = set(str(x).strip() for x in node_3_answer) & set(str(x).strip() for x in node_4_answer)
df["Year"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Year"], errors="coerce")
df["PSI"] = pd.to_numeric(df["PSI"], errors="coerce")
df["ObsRate"] = pd.to_numeric(df["ObsRate"], errors="coerce")
df["County"] = df["County"].astype(str).str.strip()
filtered = df[
    df["Year"].eq(2015)
    & df["PSI"].eq(27)
    & df["County"].isin(county_set)
    & (df["ObsRate"] >= 19)
]
answer = sorted(filtered["County"].dropna().astype(str).unique().tolist())
5.subquestion: Among the counties from hop 2, which have ObsRate >= 19 for PSI 27 (Perioperative Hemorrhage or Hematoma) in 2015? (Filter: Year == 2015; PSI == 27; County in <node_3 answer>, <node_4 answer>; read ObsRate; keep >= 19.)

import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/california-county-boundaries-bd0f6/files/California_County_Boundaries_9101614255844818638.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
node_3_answer = ['Alameda', 'Contra Costa', 'San Francisco', 'Santa Clara']
node_4_answer = ['Alameda', 'San Francisco', 'San Mateo', 'Santa Clara']


county_set = set(str(x).strip() for x in node_3_answer) & set(str(x).strip() for x in node_4_answer)
df["COUNTY_NAME"] = df["COUNTY_NAME"].astype(str).str.strip()
df["Shape__Area"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Shape__Area"], errors="coerce")
filtered = df[
    df["COUNTY_NAME"].isin(county_set)
    & (df["Shape__Area"] >= 3000000000)
]
answer = sorted(filtered["COUNTY_NAME"].dropna().astype(str).unique().tolist())
6.subquestion: Among the counties from hop 2, which have Shape__Area >= 3000000000 in the California county boundaries data? (Filter: COUNTY_NAME in <node_3 answer>, <node_4 answer>; read Shape__Area; keep >= 3000000000.)
